title: Cancel booking: AI refuses direct cancellation after booking confirmed
failing_behavior: After booking was confirmed via _submitDirectBooking, when customer said 'cancel my appointment', the AI responded 'I'm not able to cancel appointments directly. Please call the salon.' The booking was never cancelled in Firestore.
root_cause: Three missing pieces: (1) _submitDirectBooking cleared biz._bookingState before running, and didn't restore name/phone — so Claude's next turn had empty STATE and couldn't identify the customer. (2) There was no CANCEL BOOKING section in the system prompt. (3) _parseEscalationType didn't include 'cancel'. All three had to be present for cancel to work.

conversation.content: I'd like to cancel my appointment

precondition: biz._bookingState = {services: ['Manicure'], staff: 'Helen', name: 'Jane Smith', phone: '4085551234', lang: 'en'}. biz._lastBookingId = 'DLC-TEST99'. This state is set by _submitDirectBooking after a prior booking.
notes: The cancel flow requires all three pieces to be in place simultaneously. Breaking any one of them silently disables cancel. The invariant memory file (booking_cancel_modify_flow.md) documents the five invariants that must stay intact.
